title: Form follows function for RNA editing - Nature Biotechnology
gist: Summarizing work by Song et al., this piece describes how using AlphaFold 3 to study ADAR's binding mechanism to double-stranded RNA enabled the design of improved ADAR-recruiting RNAs (arRNAs), with strategic placement of RNA structural bulges reducing bystander editing and extending the usable target range for endogenous ADAR-based A-to-I RNA editing.

insights.claim: A-to-I RNA editing using endogenous ADAR is considered a therapeutically promising, safer alternative to permanent DNA editing, but the approach remains limited by widespread bystander editing and by target restrictions arising from ADAR's intrinsic sequence preferences.
insights.speaker: Alexandra Despang
insights.so_what: Because RNA editing is pitched as a safer, non-permanent alternative to DNA editing, solving its bystander-editing and sequence-restriction limitations directly expands the addressable target list for RNA-editing therapeutics developers (e.g., companies building ADAR-recruiting RNA platforms).
insights.quote: While advantageous, this strategy remains limited by widespread bystander editing and target restrictions due to ADAR’s intrinsic sequence preferences.

insights.claim: Song et al. used AlphaFold 3 to systematically study how ADAR binds double-stranded RNA and found that only a small segment of the extended RNA structure is catalytically relevant within a defined interaction window.
insights.speaker: Alexandra Despang
insights.so_what: This is a concrete example of AlphaFold-derived structural insight being applied to redesign a gene/RNA-editing therapeutic tool, evidence of AI-driven structural biology directly accelerating platform engineering in the RNA-editing field.

insights.claim: By testing various positions of RNA bulges within the ADAR-recruiting RNA (arRNA) design, the authors improved precision by placing bulges within ADAR's binding regions and separately improved efficiency by positioning bulges in the peripheral ADAR-binding areas.
insights.speaker: Alexandra Despang
insights.so_what: This gives RNA-editing tool developers a specific, actionable design rule — bulge placement relative to the ADAR-binding window — for independently tuning precision versus efficiency in their own arRNA constructs.
insights.locator: After examining the effect of RNA structure
